description: Complete step-by-step release process. Every release — no matter how small — follows every step in order. No step is optional.

agent_instructions.role: You are a release engineer executing the OpenHomeHub release process. Work through every phase and every step in exact order. Do not proceed to the next step until the current step's verify command returns the expected output. If any step fails, stop and report the failure before continuing.
agent_instructions.python_environment: source backend/.venv/bin/activate before any Python/alembic/pytest command
agent_instructions.version_format: MAJOR.MINOR — always two decimal places e.g. 0.10, 0.11, 1.00. Increment MINOR by 1 per release (0.10 → 0.11). A MAJOR bump resets MINOR to 0.
agent_instructions.canonical_version_file: backend/app/core/config.py — the APP_VERSION constant is the single source of truth
agent_instructions.abort_rule: If any test fails or any verify command returns unexpected output, STOP the release. Fix the issue and restart from Phase 3 (tests).

phases.title: Pre-Release: Check GitHub for Issues
phases.description: Before touching any code, check what the community has reported. This drives the TODO and FUTURE_ENHANCEMENTS files.

steps.title: Fetch open GitHub issues via API
commands: # Fetch all open issues from the repo (replace vnmyers13/openhomehub)

python3 -c "import json; issues=json.load(open('/tmp/gh_issues.json')); [print(f\"#{i['number']} [{i['labels'][0]['name'] if i['labels'] else 'unlabeled'}] {i['title']} — {i['html_url']}\") for i in issues]"
steps.verify: cat /tmp/gh_issues.json | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(f'Fetched {len(d)} open issues')"
steps.note: If GITHUB_TOKEN is set, add -H 'Authorization: token $GITHUB_TOKEN' for private rate limits

steps.title: Categorise issues into bugs vs enhancements
commands: bugs = [i for i in issues if any(l['name'].lower() in ['bug','defect','error'] for l in i['labels'])]
enhancements = [i for i in issues if any(l['name'].lower() in ['enhancement','feature','feature request'] for l in i['labels'])]
unlabeled = [i for i in issues if not i['labels']]
steps.verify: test -f /tmp/new_bugs.txt && echo BUGS_FILE_OK

steps.title: Merge new issues into TODO.md and FUTURE_ENHANCEMENTS.md
commands: # Append new bugs to TODO.md (avoiding duplicates by issue number)
if num and num.group(1) not in existing_nums:
print('No new bugs to add to TODO.md')
if num and num.group(1) not in existing_nums:
steps.verify: test -f TODO.md && test -f FUTURE_ENHANCEMENTS.md && echo FILES_OK

phases.title: Version Bump
phases.description: Increment the version number in every location that records it. One command does all of them.

steps.title: Read current version
commands: python3 -c "from app.core.config import settings; print('Current version:', settings.app_version)" 2>/dev/null || grep 'APP_VERSION' backend/app/core/config.py
steps.verify: grep 'APP_VERSION' backend/app/core/config.py | grep -E '[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' && echo VERSION_FOUND

steps.title: Increment version by 0.01 in all canonical locations
commands: # Read current version from canonical source
raise ValueError('Could not find APP_VERSION in config.py')
# Files that contain the version string
print(f'  WARN {filepath} — old version string not found, skipping')
print('ACTION REQUIRED: Edit CHANGELOG.md and fill in the release notes before committing.')
steps.verify: grep 'APP_VERSION' backend/app/core/config.py | python3 -c "import sys,re; line=sys.stdin.read(); m=re.search(r'APP_VERSION.*([0-9]+)\.([0-9]+)',line); print('New version:', m.group(0) if m else 'NOT FOUND')"
steps.note: After this step: open CHANGELOG.md and fill in the release notes before proceeding

steps.title: Update CHANGELOG.md with release notes
steps.instruction: Open CHANGELOG.md in your editor. Fill in the three sections (Changed, Fixed, Added) for this release. Do not leave placeholder text. Save the file before proceeding.
steps.verify: python3 -c "import pathlib; text=pathlib.Path('CHANGELOG.md').read_text(); assert 'describe changes here' not in text, 'CHANGELOG still has placeholder text'; print('CHANGELOG OK')"
steps.note: This step cannot be automated — it requires human judgement about what changed

phases.title: Documentation Update
phases.description: Every user-facing document must be accurate before the release goes out.

steps.title: Verify Dockerfile version label matches new version
commands: if not ver_match: raise ValueError('No APP_VERSION in config.py')
if ver not in dockerfile: print(f'WARNING: Version {ver} not in Dockerfile')
steps.verify: grep 'LABEL version' backend/Dockerfile || grep 'VERSION' backend/Dockerfile | head -1

steps.title: Verify README version badge / quick-start version reference
commands: if count == 0: print('WARNING: README does not mention new version')
steps.verify: grep -c '[0-9]\.[0-9][0-9]' README.md | python3 -c "import sys; n=int(sys.stdin.read()); print(f'README has {n} version references'); assert n>=1" && echo README_OK

steps.title: Verify API docs endpoint reflects new version
commands: # Start the API locally and check /api/health returns the new version
steps.verify: python3 -c "import re, pathlib; ver=re.search(r\"APP_VERSION.*['\"](.*?)['\"]\",pathlib.Path('backend/app/core/config.py').read_text()).group(1); print(f'Expecting version {ver} from /api/health')"

steps.title: Check that GitHub repo description and topics are current
steps.instruction: Navigate to https://github.com/vnmyers13/openhomehub. Click the gear icon next to About. Verify: (1) Description is accurate, (2) Website field is correct if applicable, (3) Topics include: self-hosted, docker, raspberry-pi, family-calendar, fastapi, react, pwa. Update any that are stale. Screenshot or note the current description for the release notes.
steps.verify: echo 'MANUAL_STEP — confirm GitHub description reviewed before continuing'

steps.title: Verify all environment variable documentation is current
commands: # Get all env var names referenced in config.py
# Find field names in the Settings class
field_names = [f.upper() for f in field_names if not f.startswith('_')]
missing = [f for f in field_names if f not in env_example]
print(f'WARNING: These fields are in config.py but not in .env.example:')
print(f'All {len(field_names)} config fields are documented in .env.example')
steps.verify: test -f .env.example && echo ENV_EXAMPLE_EXISTS

phases.title: Test Suite — Full Run
phases.description: Every test must pass. Zero failures, zero errors. No skipped tests without documented reason.

steps.title: Install/sync all backend dependencies into venv
steps.verify: source backend/.venv/bin/activate && pip check && echo DEPS_OK

steps.title: Run backend unit and integration tests
steps.verify: python3 -c "output=open('/tmp/pytest_output.txt').read(); assert 'failed' not in output.lower() or '0 failed' in output, 'TESTS FAILED — see /tmp/pytest_output.txt'; passed=__import__('re').search(r'(\d+) passed',output); print(f'Tests: {passed.group(0) if passed else \"unknown\"}'); print('ALL TESTS PASS')"
steps.on_failure: STOP RELEASE. Fix all failing tests before continuing. Do not proceed with a broken test suite.

steps.title: Run database migration round-trip test
steps.verify: echo 'Migration round-trip test complete — all three steps must print OK above'

steps.title: Frontend type check
commands: cd frontend && npm run build 2>&1 | tee /tmp/frontend_build.txt
steps.verify: python3 -c "output=open('/tmp/frontend_build.txt').read(); assert 'error' not in output.lower() or 'TypeScript' not in output, 'FRONTEND BUILD ERRORS — see /tmp/frontend_build.txt'; print('Frontend build OK')"
steps.on_failure: STOP RELEASE. Fix all TypeScript/build errors before continuing.

steps.title: Docker Compose build test (both images)
steps.verify: python3 -c "output=open('/tmp/docker_build.txt').read(); assert 'ERROR' not in output, 'DOCKER BUILD FAILED — see /tmp/docker_build.txt'; print('Docker build OK')"
steps.on_failure: STOP RELEASE. Fix Docker build errors before continuing.

steps.title: Smoke test: docker compose up and health check
steps.verify: docker compose up -d && sleep 8 && curl -sk https://openhomehub.local/api/health | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); assert d['status']=='ok'; print('Smoke test OK — version:', d.get('version','?'))" && docker compose down

steps.title: Record test results
commands: - pytest: {passed.group(0) if passed else 'unknown'} {'FAILED: ' + failed.group(0) if failed else '0 failed'}
steps.verify: test -f docs/test-history.md && echo TEST_HISTORY_RECORDED

phases.title: Git Commit & Tag
phases.description: Commit all changes with a structured message and create a version tag.

steps.title: Stage all changed files
steps.verify: git status | grep -E 'modified|new file' && echo 'Files staged — review above before proceeding'

steps.title: Final review of staged changes
steps.instruction: Review the git diff output above. Confirm: (1) No .env file is staged (it must never be committed). (2) No test database files (*.db) are staged. (3) CHANGELOG.md has real release notes (no placeholders). (4) Version number in config.py matches the expected new version. Only then continue.
steps.verify: git diff --cached --name-only | python3 -c "import sys; files=sys.stdin.read(); assert '.env' not in files, 'ERROR: .env is staged — unstage it immediately'; assert '.db' not in files, 'ERROR: .db file is staged'; print('Staged files clean — no secrets or databases')"

steps.title: Commit with structured message
commands: See CHANGELOG.md for full release notes."
steps.verify: git log --oneline -1 | grep 'release:' && echo COMMIT_OK

steps.title: Create annotated version tag
steps.verify: git tag --sort=-creatordate | head -1 | python3 -c "import sys; tag=sys.stdin.read().strip(); assert tag.startswith('v'), f'Latest tag {tag} does not start with v'; print(f'Tag verified: {tag}')"

phases.title: Publish to GitHub
phases.description: Push the commit and tag to GitHub. Create a GitHub Release with the CHANGELOG notes.

steps.title: Push commits and tags to origin
steps.verify: git log origin/main --oneline -1 | grep 'release:' && echo PUSH_OK

steps.title: Verify GitHub Actions CI passes on the new commit
commands: # Poll GitHub Actions until the workflow completes
print('Wait for the build workflow to complete before proceeding.')
steps.verify: echo 'MANUAL_CHECK: Navigate to https://github.com/vnmyers13/openhomehub/actions and confirm the latest workflow run shows a green checkmark before proceeding'

steps.title: Create GitHub Release with CHANGELOG notes
commands: # Read the top entry from CHANGELOG.md
# Extract the first release block (between first ## and second ##)
# Create the release using GitHub CLI (if installed)

gh release create "v${NEW_VER}" --title "OpenHomeHub v${NEW_VER}" --notes-file /tmp/release_notes.md 2>/dev/null || echo 'gh CLI not found — create the release manually at https://github.com/vnmyers13/openhomehub/releases/new'
steps.verify: echo 'Verify GitHub Release is visible at https://github.com/vnmyers13/openhomehub/releases'

phases.title: Publish to Docker Hub
phases.description: Build production images and push to Docker Hub with version and latest tags.

steps.title: Log in to Docker Hub
steps.verify: docker info 2>/dev/null | grep 'Username' && echo DOCKERHUB_LOGIN_OK
steps.note: Set DOCKERHUB_USERNAME env var before running. Password will be prompted interactively. Use a Docker Hub access token (not your account password) — generate one at https://hub.docker.com/settings/security

steps.title: Build production images with version tag
steps.verify: NEW_VER=$(cat /tmp/new_version.txt) && docker images | grep openhomehub | grep ${NEW_VER} && echo IMAGES_BUILT_OK

steps.title: Smoke test the production Docker images
commands: # Quick sanity check: API image starts and returns health
steps.verify: docker run --rm -d --name fh_ver_check -e SECRET_KEY=testsecretkey12345678901234567890123456789012 -e DATABASE_URL=sqlite+aiosqlite:////tmp/test.db -p 8098:8000 ${DOCKERHUB_USERNAME}/openhomehub-api:latest && sleep 6 && curl -s http://localhost:8098/api/health | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); assert d['status']=='ok'; print('Docker image smoke test PASS, version:', d.get('version','?'))" && docker stop fh_ver_check

steps.title: Push images to Docker Hub
steps.verify: NEW_VER=$(cat /tmp/new_version.txt) && echo 'Verify at: https://hub.docker.com/r/'${DOCKERHUB_USERNAME}'/openhomehub-api/tags'

steps.title: Update Docker Hub README / description
commands: # Push README to Docker Hub API (requires DOCKERHUB_TOKEN)
print('DOCKERHUB_TOKEN not set — update Docker Hub description manually at:')
steps.verify: echo 'Docker Hub README update attempted — verify manually if DOCKERHUB_TOKEN was not set'

phases.title: Post-Release Housekeeping
phases.description: Close the loop — update tracking files and confirm everything is in order.

steps.title: Update TODO.md — mark resolved items
steps.instruction: Open TODO.md. For every item that was fixed in this release: change '- [ ]' to '- [x]' and append '(fixed in vX.XX)'. Move the completed items to a 'Completed' section at the bottom of the file. Save.
steps.verify: grep -c '\- \[x\]' TODO.md | python3 -c "import sys; n=int(sys.stdin.read()); print(f'{n} items marked complete in TODO.md')" || echo 'No completed items yet — OK for first release'

steps.title: Write release summary to docs/releases/
commands: See docs/test-history.md for full test output.
- [x] GitHub issues reviewed and TODO/FUTURE_ENHANCEMENTS updated
- [x] Docker images built and smoke tested
- [x] Pushed to Docker Hub with version and latest tags
steps.verify: ls docs/releases/*.md | head -1 && echo RELEASE_SUMMARY_OK

steps.title: Commit housekeeping files
commands: git add TODO.md FUTURE_ENHANCEMENTS.md docs/test-history.md docs/releases/
steps.verify: git log --oneline -1 | grep 'housekeeping' && echo HOUSEKEEPING_COMMITTED

steps.title: Announce release (optional)
steps.instruction: If the project has a Discord, Slack, or community forum: post a brief release announcement with the version number and the key changes from CHANGELOG.md. Link to the GitHub Release page.
steps.verify: echo 'MANUAL_STEP — optional announcement'

full_checklist_order: P1: Check GitHub issues → update TODO.md + FUTURE_ENHANCEMENTS.md
P2: Bump version in config.py, Dockerfile, README, CHANGELOG
P3: Verify all documentation is accurate
P4: Run full test suite — ALL must pass before continuing
P5: git commit with structured message + annotated tag
P6: Push to GitHub + wait for CI + create GitHub Release
P7: Build Docker images + smoke test + push to Docker Hub
P8: Mark resolved TODOs + write release summary + housekeeping commit

required_env_vars_for_release.GITHUB_TOKEN: Optional — increases GitHub API rate limit for issue fetching

files_that_record_version: backend/Dockerfile — LABEL version= or ENV VERSION=
README.md — version badge or quick-start reference
